fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), which incorrectly allows a new action when the number of active events is exactly equal to the limit. It should use greater-than-or-equal (`>=`) to deny access when the limit has been reached.

constraints.intent: The number of active events should not be exactly equal to the limit when deciding whether to allow the action.
If the request is allowed, the number of active events must be strictly less than the limit.

spec.expr: all(ts > cutoff__1 for ts in active__1)

constraints.intent: All timestamps in the active set must be more recent than the cutoff time.
The 'allowed' status must be false if and only if active events are at or over the limit.
